message:
fix(safety): eliminate unsafe transmute and mem::forget patterns across codebase

Replace all unsafe memory operations with safe, idiomatic Rust
alternatives throughout the codebase. These changes remove potential
undefined behavior from invalid discriminant values and ensure proper
resource cleanup without relying on mem::forget.

vtparse enums (frankenterm/vtparse/src/enums.rs):
- Replace unsafe core::mem::transmute(v) in Action::from_u16() and
  State::from_u16() with safe const lookup-table decoding using
  ACTION_BY_U16 and STATE_BY_U16 arrays
- Invalid discriminants now panic with a clear message instead of
  triggering undefined behavior
- Add tests verifying the panic behavior on invalid values

cell bitfields (frankenterm/cell/src/lib.rs):
- Replace unsafe mem::transmute in the bitfield! macro with a safe
  BitfieldEnumDecode trait
- Implement from_bits() for Intensity, Underline, Blink, SemanticType,
  and VerticalAlign with explicit match arms that fall back to defaults
  for invalid bit patterns instead of producing UB
- Add test confirming invalid enum values produce safe defaults

filedescriptor unix (frankenterm/filedescriptor/src/unix.rs):
- Replace std::mem::forget(self) in OwnedHandle::into_raw_fd() with
  ManuallyDrop::new(self) — the idiomatic safe pattern to prevent the
  Drop impl from running without leaking the intent

filedescriptor windows (frankenterm/filedescriptor/src/windows.rs):
- Same mem::forget to ManuallyDrop fix for OwnedHandle::into_raw_handle()
- Replace three std::mem::transmute calls in socketpair_impl() with
  explicit pointer casts (as *const _ / as *mut _), eliminating unsound
  transmute of SOCKADDR_IN to raw pointers

snapshot_engine bench + proptest_session_restore:
- Replace std::mem::forget(dir) with let _ = dir.keep() to persist
  tempdir without leaking the handle — preserves test artifacts while
  properly releasing the TempDir guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
